title: corethrogyne filaginifolia var. bernardina
intro: Corethrogyne filaginifolia var. bernardina, also known as the San Diego everlasting or bush sunflower, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin conditions, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Corethrogyne filaginifolia var. bernardina is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
